ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application shall comply with the corporate standards for user interface creation for internally and externally used applications.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application shall interface with the Cardmember Information Database. ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes applications shall interface with the Merchant Information Database. The Merchant Information Database provides detailed information with regard to the merchant. All merchant detail information shall be obtained from the Merchant Information Database.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes application must maintain a detailed history of every action that a user takes on a dispute case. This ensures a complete audit trail if questions arise later on with regard to a particular dispute case.

ground_truth_requirements.text: All actions that modify an existing dispute case must be recorded in the case history.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must be accessible by both internal and external users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must prevent users from accessing any dispute cases that do not belong to their cardholder base.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System will facilitate direct data entry of a dispute case via a user interface that supports real time responses to the users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide different levels of access with regard to disputes case initiation and follow-up actions.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall provide view access capability for authorized users of the application.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall provide update access capability for authorized users of the application.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must allow the users to select disputable transactions (based on the age of the transaction) from a user interface and initiate a dispute (ticket retrieval request or chargeback notification) on the selected transaction.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide the user the ability to initiate a single dispute case on multiple transactions that belong to a single merchant.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System will provide the user the ability to create or initiate a ticket retrieval request. As part of ticket retrieval creation process the system must prompt the user to enter all the required information to create the ticket retrieval request. The ticket retrieval request is a document that is sent to merchant inquiring the validity of a transaction.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must allow the user to create three unique types of ticket retrieval requests. The three types of ticket retrieval requests are (1) Request for original receipt (2) Request for a copy of the receipt or (3) Request for a portfolio. A portfolio consists of documentation that would provide proof of a purchase such as the documentation that is received from a car rental agency that is more than a sales receipt.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must prevent external users from requesting original receipts. Requests for original receipts are restricted to internal users.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide a confirmation to the user upon the creation of ticket retrieval request that contains the following information; the dispute case number the type of retrieval requested (copy original or portfolio) and the date that the merchant response is due.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall allow the user to create or initiate a chargeback request. The system must prompt the user to enter the required information to initiate a chargeback request. The chargeback request results in a printed chargeback notification that is sent to merchant indicating that their account will be charged back if no proof is provided that the disputed transaction is valid.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide a confirmation to the user upon the creation of a chargeback request. The confirmation must contain the following information; the dispute case number the type of chargeback requested (pending or immediate) and the date that the merchant response is due.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall prevent the creation of duplicate dispute requests 100% of the time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall accurately provide a list of possible reason codes for a given transaction. The list of possible reason codes must be derived based on the type of transaction and the industry that the merchant belongs to according to the business rules defined for the Disputes System.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide the ability to view existing dispute cases.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide Search functionality. The search method must include the ability to search by (1) the dispute case number (2) the merchant account number (3) the cardmember account number and (4) the issuer number. In addition to the above criteria the search functionality must further allow the user to limit the results of the search by a date range the type of dispute (ticket retrieval request or chargeback notification) the case status (open closed or all) and the dispute reason code.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: A list of all disputes cases that meet the entered search criteria must be provided to the user. The list of cases must differentiate the type and/or status of the case by color -coding the line item in the list. For example all open cases will be yellow and all closed cases will be gray.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall provide the users the ability to view the history of all activities that have occurred on the dispute case.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Dispute System shall provide the users with the ability to follow up on a dispute case via follow-up actions. The list of available follow up actions for a given dispute case must be filtered by the status of the case and the access level of the user.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall record the name of the user and the date for any activity that creates or modifies the disputes case in the system. A detailed history of the actions taken on the case including the date and the user that performed the action must be maintained for auditing purposes.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: For any systematic (non-user initiated) action that occurs on a case such as the disputes aging process a case activity will be generated. Upon the next logon the user that initiated the dispute case must be notified that a systematic action has occurred on the dispute case.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: All letter requests must be formatted according to guidelines specified by the Print Letter Utility system.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Any disputes cases that have been closed for over 6 months must be purged from the online disputes database.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall gracefully handle failures or timeouts from the ChoiceParts external data feed by displaying a clear error message to the estimator and allowing the estimate to be saved in a pending state for later completion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 500 concurrent users across all insurance company tenants without degradation in response time beyond defined performance thresholds.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on standard web browsers without requiring client-side software installation, to support use in both office environments and repair shop environments with varying IT infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow supervisory adjusters to view the historical rating record and associated audit reports for any repair facility within their insurance company's network.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall follow a modular architecture with documented interfaces between components, enabling individual modules (e.g., ChoiceParts integration, reporting, RBAC) to be updated or replaced independently.
generated_requirements.rationale: The system integrates with an external data feed and serves multiple tenants; a modular, well-documented architecture reduces the cost and risk of future changes or integration updates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow an administrator to onboard a new insurance company tenant by creating an isolated data partition, configuring company-specific preferred supplier lists, and provisioning initial user roles without requiring code changes or system downtime.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall transmit all data between clients and the server, and between the server and the ChoiceParts data feed, exclusively over encrypted channels using TLS 1.2 or higher.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically generate Inventory Quantity Adjustment (IQA) documents by calculating ingredient consumption based on daily sales data and the corresponding product formulas.
generated_requirements.rationale: This is the core automation feature described in the use case — deriving inventory adjustments from sales and formulas.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow formula administrators to create, read, update, and delete product formulas, including support for multi-level semi-product components within a formula.
generated_requirements.rationale: Formula management with semi-product support is explicitly required for staff to define how finished products are composed.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the definition of one or more substitutionary ingredients for any ingredient within a product formula, and shall apply substitutions automatically during IQA generation when the primary ingredient stock is insufficient.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ingredient substitution is explicitly called out in the use case as a factor in IQA calculation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow inventory managers to manually create, review, approve, and void Inventory Quantity Adjustment documents.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inventory managers need full lifecycle control over adjustment documents to maintain accurate stock records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized staff to build and organize product menus in a hierarchical (tree) structure, with support for categories, sub-categories, and individual menu items.
generated_requirements.rationale: Hierarchical menu management is explicitly described as a requirement for organizing products for POS integration.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronize the hierarchical product menu with connected POS terminals, ensuring that any menu changes are reflected on POS devices within 5 minutes of being published.
generated_requirements.rationale: POS integration is a stated requirement; timely synchronization ensures operational accuracy at the point of sale.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC), restricting access to features and data based on the user's assigned role (e.g., inventory manager, formula administrator, POS operator).
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-based access control is explicitly mandated in the use case to protect sensitive operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, tamper-evident audit log of all create, update, delete, and approval actions performed on formulas, menus, and inventory adjustment documents, recording the user identity, timestamp, and before/after values.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit-compliant accounting standards are explicitly required; a detailed audit trail is necessary to satisfy this constraint.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall consider current on-hand inventory levels when generating IQA documents, ensuring that adjustments do not produce negative stock quantities unless explicitly overridden by an authorized user.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case states that current inventory levels must be accounted for during IQA generation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 50 concurrent authenticated users without degradation in response time.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case specifies multiple concurrent users; a concrete concurrency threshold ensures the system is sized appropriately.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete the automatic generation of an IQA document for a single business day's sales data within 30 seconds, regardless of the number of product lines processed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely IQA generation is operationally critical; a response-time bound prevents unacceptable delays in daily closing workflows.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmissions between the system and POS terminals shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protecting sales and inventory data in transit is essential in a multi-terminal restaurant environment.

generated_requirements.text: User passwords shall be stored using a salted cryptographic hash (e.g., bcrypt or Argon2), and the system shall enforce a minimum password complexity policy of at least 8 characters including uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character.
generated_requirements.rationale: Secure credential storage and strong password policies are baseline security requirements for a multi-user business system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall run on the Windows operating system (Windows 10 or later) and shall operate on existing restaurant hardware without requiring hardware upgrades.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly states the system must run in a Windows-based environment on existing hardware.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of at least 99.5% during defined business hours (06:00–24:00 local time), excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Restaurant operations are time-critical; high availability during business hours prevents revenue loss and operational disruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall follow consistent visual and interaction patterns such that a new staff member with basic computer literacy can complete core tasks (formula entry, menu update, IQA review) after no more than 2 hours of training.
generated_requirements.rationale: Restaurant staff may have varying technical backgrounds; a low training threshold reduces onboarding cost and errors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed with a modular architecture, and each module (formula management, menu management, inventory adjustment, POS integration) shall be independently deployable and testable.
generated_requirements.rationale: A modular design reduces the cost and risk of future changes, bug fixes, and feature additions to individual subsystems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover automatically from a transient database connection failure within 60 seconds without data loss or corruption to any in-progress transaction.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transient failures in a networked restaurant environment are common; automatic recovery protects data integrity and operational continuity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall import daily sales data from POS terminals in CSV or JSON format, validating the structure and content of the file before processing and rejecting malformed records with a descriptive error report.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reliable ingestion of POS sales data is a prerequisite for accurate automated IQA generation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dashboard view for inventory managers displaying current stock levels, pending IQA documents, and alerts for ingredients that have fallen below a configurable reorder threshold.
generated_requirements.rationale: A consolidated dashboard supports proactive inventory management and aligns with the inventory manager role described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow players to register a unique username and create an account to access the online lobby.
generated_requirements.rationale: Player registration is the entry point to the game; unique usernames prevent identity conflicts in the shared lobby.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a shared lobby where all registered and currently available players are listed, allowing any player to select an available opponent and initiate a game.
generated_requirements.rationale: The lobby is the matchmaking mechanism described in the use case, enabling players to find and challenge each other.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present each player with a personal grid on which they must secretly position their fleet of ships before the game begins, with ship placements hidden from the opponent.
generated_requirements.rationale: Secret ship placement on a personal defensive grid is a core rule of Battleship gameplay.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce strict turn-based progression, allowing only the active player to fire a shot at any given time, and automatically transferring the turn to the opponent after each shot is resolved.
generated_requirements.rationale: Turn-based mechanics are fundamental to Battleship; automatic turn management prevents rule violations and keeps the game fair.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record and display the outcome of each shot — hit, miss, or sunk — on both the attacking player's opponent grid view and the defending player's own grid view in real time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Tracking and communicating shot outcomes is essential for both players to understand the game state and make strategic decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect when all ships in a player's fleet have been sunk and immediately declare the opposing player as the winner, ending the game session.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic win detection is required to conclude the game correctly without relying on manual player input.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow any player to forfeit an active game at any time, which shall immediately end the game session and declare the non-forfeiting player as the winner.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly mentions forfeit as a valid game-ending condition; this must be handled gracefully.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render the game interface with a nautical theme, including a periscope-style framing of the game grids and realistic ship imagery on the placement and tracking grids.
generated_requirements.rationale: The nautical visual theme is an explicit design requirement described in the use case and contributes to the intended user experience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall play contextually appropriate sound effects for gameplay events, including distinct audio cues for a hit, a miss, and a ship sinking.
generated_requirements.rationale: Sound effects for hits, misses, and sinkings are explicitly called out in the use case as part of the gameplay experience.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support English, Spanish, and French as selectable interface languages, applying the selected language to all UI text, labels, messages, and notifications.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-language support for English, Spanish, and French is explicitly required to serve the described broad international audience.

generated_requirements.text: All real-time game state updates — including shot outcomes and turn changes — shall be reflected on both players' screens within 500 milliseconds of the triggering action under normal network conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time multiplayer gameplay requires low-latency synchronization to maintain a fair and engaging experience for both players.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 1,000 concurrent game sessions without degradation of response time or game state consistency.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case specifies the system must be highly concurrent to serve a broad international audience simultaneously.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.9% uptime on a 24/7 basis, measured on a rolling 30-day window.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case states the system is designed to be continuously available, requiring a quantified high-availability target.

generated_requirements.text: All player authentication credentials shall be stored using a salted cryptographic hashing algorithm (e.g., bcrypt or Argon2), and all client-server communication shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protecting player account credentials and in-transit game data is a baseline security requirement for any internet-facing multiplayer system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent players from accessing or inferring the opponent's ship positions through any client-side data, API response, or network traffic inspection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Hidden ship placement is a core game rule; exposing opponent positions via the network would fundamentally break game integrity.

generated_requirements.text: A new user with no prior experience of the system shall be able to complete registration, enter the lobby, set up ships, and take their first shot without consulting any external documentation, as validated by usability testing with a representative sample of new users.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly states the system must be intuitive enough for new users to play without any prior training.

generated_requirements.text: If a network interruption occurs during an active game session, the system shall preserve the full game state for a minimum of 5 minutes, allowing a disconnected player to reconnect and resume without loss of progress.
generated_requirements.rationale: Network interruptions are common in internet play; preserving game state protects the experience of both players and prevents unfair game terminations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain all processed messages in a persistent store for audit and transaction tracking purposes, with retention policies configurable by authorized administrators.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall follow consistent layout and interaction patterns so that a new finance staff member can complete a standard budget submission without formal training beyond a one-page quick-reference guide.
generated_requirements.rationale: Finance staff across multiple organizational levels must be able to use the system efficiently; poor usability risks errors in financial submissions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support concurrent usage by at least 200 simultaneous users without degradation of response time beyond the defined performance thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: A multi-level organizational hierarchy (branch, complex, district, regional) implies a large user base that will be active simultaneously during the budgeting cycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall be structured in modular, well-documented components such that annual updates to budget formulae can be applied by a developer within one business day without changes to other system modules.
generated_requirements.rationale: Formulae are updated annually before each budgeting cycle; the system must support rapid, low-risk updates to these calculations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from an unplanned outage and restore full service within 4 hours, with no loss of committed (saved) budget submission data.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability and data integrity are critical for a financial budgeting system; data loss during the budgeting cycle would have significant business impact.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronize with the BDW on a scheduled basis (at minimum nightly) and provide a manual refresh option for administrators to trigger an on-demand data reload.
generated_requirements.rationale: Keeping BDW-sourced revenue and expense data current is essential for accurate budget calculations; both scheduled and on-demand sync are needed.

ground_truth_requirements.text: The RFS system should be available 24/7 especially during the budgeting period. The RFS system shall be available 90% of the time all year and 98% during the budgeting period. 2% of the time the system will become available within 1 hour of the time that the situation is reported.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 98% of the user workstations shall meet the minimum hardware requirements prior to implementation. Minimum Hard Disk Space: 400MB.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: 98% of the user workstations shall meet the minimum software requirements prior to implementation: Microsoft Internet Explorer 5.0. Microsoft .NET Framework 1.1. Microsoft Internet Explorer Web Controls
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The RFS system should be able to easily interface with the BDW environment in order to load the data. Seven data files shall be received from the BDW and loaded into the RFS system within 3 hours one day prior to the start of budgeting process every year. The data will be as of October month-end. Test files (7) shall be loaded up to 5 times throughout the year from the BDW.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Changes to revenue and expense formulae must be applied to the RFS system every year prior to the budgeting process start. Revenue and expense formulae will be updated within one week of their receipt after which 2 weeks will be allotted to testing of the budget calculations to be conducted jointly by the project development team and PCG Finance.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: New branches/complexes/districts/regions and revenue/expense accounts added to the BDW must be reflected in the RFS system. 100% of the entities (mentioned above) available as of October month-end will be loaded into the RFS system prior to the budget start process.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Only authorized user shall access the RFS system. ONLY users that exist in the UAS system with the RFS system assigned to their role(s) shall access the RFS system and use it.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: User access should be limited to the permissions granted to their role(s) Each level in the PCG hierarchy will be assigned a role and users will be assigned to these roles. Access to functionality within RFS system is dependent on the privileges/permission assigned to the role.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product must comply with the intranet page standards and requirements of ENET Securities. 95% of the product look & feel will be approved by the Architecture group prior to implementation. The remaining 5% will be corrected and approved within one month of product release.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The intranet pages should display appropriately in all resolutions from 800x600 and higher. The display of all the pages will be correct in 95% of the resolutions prior to implementation. The remaining 5% will be corrected within a week of notification of the incorrect display.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The users should be able to easily use the system to successfully complete their budgets within the stipulated time for completion. With a week of training prior to product implementation 98% of the users shall complete their budgets within the stipulated time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product interface should be fast. The response time of the product interface should not exceed 2 seconds 90% of the time. For 10% of the time the response time should not be more than 10 seconds.not be more than 10 seconds.
ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR
